mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recessionary pressures, which systematically reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer spending and capital investment, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher near-term equity returns during established expansion phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of positive INDPRO growth observations, or the signal reverses direction during supply-chain disruption episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 3.5 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, producing negative forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of high-inflation observations, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in rolling window regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarter-over-quarter trend in real GDP growth reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of positive GDP growth quarters, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production combined with elevated inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns during monetary tightening phases.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample predictive accuracy over single-series baselines.
